fedEvents.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
fedEvents.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

fedEvents.title: FedEx posts strong earnings results in last quarter with freight business
fedEvents.content: FedEx posted strong fiscal fourth quarter earnings on Tuesday in the company's last quarter that included the freight business before its spin-off.

fedEvents.title: CFTC sues Kentucky over actions against prediction markets, making it first red state to face federal scrutiny
fedEvents.content: The commission has now sued nine states in its battle to defend what it sees as its exclusive right to regulate event contracts.

fedEvents.title: Inside Kevin Warsh's selection process for the next Atlanta Fed president
fedEvents.content: Choosing a new head for the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta is an opportunity for new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh to reshape the central bank.

items.title: World’s hottest stock market rallies after 10% plunge
items.content: Chip makers Samsung and SK Hynix recover some ground after bout of profit taking

items.title: Trump may say he’s banning Wall Street from buying homes. Does the bipartisan housing bill actually do that?
items.content: The measure “will take time to meaningfully affect housing affordability and will not resolve voter frustration in that area,” analysts said.

items.title: The No. 1 NBA draft pick will make nearly $70 million as TV deals keep money flowing into the NBA
items.content: There’s so much money at stake in the NBA draft that falling a few slots could cost a player $30 million.

items.title: Alphabet’s stock is set to join the Dow, pivoting index’s industrial roots toward tech
items.content: As Alphabet rolls out more data centers — and borrowing money to do it — it can be argued that it is becoming more of an industrial company, says strategist

items.title: Cerebras delivers its first earnings report — but it’s not enough to lift the stock
items.content: Despite upbeat revenue figures, Cerebras’s stock was falling in after-hours trading.

items.title: ‘It feels like a medical miracle’: How did a single QR code coupon cut my $618 Walgreens prescription to $15?
items.content: “This medication was generic.”

items.title: SpaceX pulls off one of the biggest AI debt deals yet
items.content: The offering is set to close on Friday and help SpaceX pay off its existing debt

items.title: ‘It seems too good to be true’: At a steak-dinner retirement seminar, the guy said annuities can outperform the market. Is that true?
items.content: “He claimed that fixed-rate annuities are the sparkly, rainbow-fairyland of investments.”

items.title: I want to leave everything to my sons, but I’m terrified they’ll give it to my ex-husband. How do I prevent this?
items.content: “I would not like my money to fall into the hands of my former spouse.”

items.title: ‘We own our home outright’: I am 67 and earn $100,000. Do I take my $30,000-a-year Social Security now or wait?
items.content: “We have combined savings of $950,000 in retirement plans, Roth IRAs and Treasuries.”

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.content: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.content: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
items.content: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

items.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
items.content: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

items.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
items.content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

items.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
items.content: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

items.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
items.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

items.title: Natural-Gas Firm Diversified Energy Strikes Deal for Permian Basin Player
items.content: Maverick Natural Resources has operations in Texas and Oklahoma.

items.title: What's New This Tax Season That Can Save You Money
items.content: There are some new wrinkles, especially for those who sell things online or bought an EV.

items.title: Chinese Investors With Few Options Turn to Dividends
items.content: Companies with good payouts are now the hottest bet in China markets.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall But Direction for Long-End Yields Still Seen Upward
items.content: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield fell and ING said the the long end of the Treasury curve will continue trading at higher yields even as Trump hasn’t delivered anything to shock markets so far.

items.title: Russia's Crude Exports Fairly Stable Despite U.S. Sanctions, Goldman Says
items.content: Oil edged lower in early trade as Goldman Sachs said the latest round of U.S. sanctions against Russia haven’t had a significant impact on the country’s oil exports.

items.title: The Extra Reward for Owning Stocks Over Bonds Has Disappeared
items.content: There is little sign of crimped demand for equities among individual investors, who remain bullish after two years of blockbuster gains.

items.title: Heard on the Street's Stock-Picking Series

items.title: What Will It Take for Home Buyers to Start Asking for a Disaster Discount?
items.content: The real costs of owning a house in neighborhoods that are vulnerable to flooding and wildfires are becoming clearer.

items.title: Now Wanted in Silicon Valley: Ho-Hum Businesses With Thin Profit Margins
items.content: Venture-capital firms are bringing AI and dealmaking to accounting, property management and other unglamorous fields.

items.title: In China, a Cat-and-Mouse Game to Rein In Crypto
items.content: Recent court cases reveal how middlemen are facilitating a booming trade, eluding a crackdown.

items.title: One House, Three Owners: The Ballooning Cost of the American Dream
items.content: The story of home affordability in the U.S. told from a single front porch.

items.title: Trump Adds Fuel to Claims That Big Banks Discriminate Against Conservatives
items.content: The president has told Bank of America and JPMorgan Chase to stop cutting conservatives off from doing business.

items.title: Reid Hoffman Raises $24.6 Million for AI Cancer-Research Startup
items.content: The LinkedIn co-founder is starting Manas AI with “The Emperor of All Maladies” author Siddhartha Mukherjee.

items.title: Can an App Replace a Personal Trainer? I Tested Nearly a Dozen to Find Out
items.content: Apps can add flexibility and freshness to your routine. Just try not to get injured.

items.content: The billionaire spoke with The Wall Street Journal ahead of his coming book, “Source Code.”

items.title: Meta Spending to Soar on AI, Massive Data Center
items.content: The social-media giant plans to spend between $60 billion and $65 billion, the latest sign of tech companies’ accelerating investments into artificial intelligence.

items.title: Canada to Review Amazon Contracts After Quebec Layoffs
items.content: Canada has warned Amazon.com that it is reviewing business ties with the company’s cloud-computing unit following Amazon’s decision to shut down its warehouses in Quebec, leading to the layoff of 1,700 workers.

items.title: Nvidia Supplier SK Hynix Posts Record Profit on AI Boom
items.content: The South Korean memory-chip maker reported record quarterly and annual results after stronger-than-expected earnings for the final quarter of 2024 on robust chip demand.

items.content: OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank unveiled AI infrastructure plans at White House.

items.title: TikTok Restoring Service for U.S. Users, After Trump Signals He Will Save It
items.content: The president-elect said he will issue an executive order, and the app said it will work on a long-term solution with the incoming administration.

items.title: How People Actually Make a Living on TikTok
items.content: The platform has provided a surge in discovery and revenue for companies that may not be able to replicate it elsewhere.

items.title: What Happens Now That TikTok's Gone Dark?
items.content: The company told users the app isn’t available and told them to stay tuned. Trump said he will likely intervene.

items.title: How a Little-Known Finnish Company Became One of the World's Hottest Gadget Startups
items.content: Oura’s smart ring measures heart health and can predict when you may be getting sick

items.title: Three New York Democrats backed by Mamdani win House seat primaries; 2 incumbents lose
items.content: Two candidates backed by the Democratic Socialist of America won their primaries, a year after the DSA-backed Zohran Mamdani won New York mayor's race.

items.title: Ukraine is raising the cost of war for Russia — and testing Putin's resolve
items.content: A string of political victories and deep-strike successes has bolstered confidence in Ukraine amid its long-running war with Russia.

items.title: Oil extends decline as Trump accuses oil firms of 'gouging' consumers
items.content: U.S. President Donald Trump intensified pressure on oil companies to lower gasoline prices following the recent decline in crude.

items.title: Defense stocks plummet after Germany to scrap warship plans; Rheinmetall stock down 13%
items.content: Berlin is planning to scrap a multi-billion-euro project to build the F126 frigates, according to the Financial Times.

items.title: Defense giant KNDS plans IPO in Paris and Frankfurt
items.content: KNDS plans to list shares in Paris and Frankfurt, with current shareholders expected to sell up to 20% of existing share capital.

items.title: Asia tech stocks rebound after global rout; Samsung jumps 9%
items.content: Asia's technology stocks mostly rebounded on Wednesday amid choppy trading following a global selloff in the previous session.

items.title: Morgan Stanley doubles China humanoid robot shipment forecast as commercialization accelerates
items.content: Morgan Stanley has sharply raised its outlook for China's humanoid robotics market, as early commercial deployment in real-world scenarios accelerated.

items.title: Cerebras falls 10% after chipmaker forecasts shrinking margin in first earnings report since IPO
items.content: Cerebras went public on the Nasdaq in May, giving Wall Street access to a pureplay AI company.

items.title: VIX owner Cboe jumps into prediction markets to build on zero-day options growth
items.content: Global markets operator Cboe has launched its first prediction markets products to meet consumer demand in the fast growing sector.

items.title: Mamdani-backed candidates are likely to win in NYC primaries, prediction market traders expect
items.content: Voters are heading to the polls in New York, with four competitive races in Democratic congressional primaries in focus.

items.title: Five things to watch in Tuesday's primaries as New York races take center stage
items.content: Tuesday’s primaries in New York, Maryland and Utah test outside money, party power and the influence of Zohran Mamdani and Donald Trump.

items.title: SpaceX raises $25 billion in debt sale less than two weeks after IPO
items.content: SpaceX raised $25 billion in a debt sale, after seeing nearly $90 billion worth of orders, sources say.

items.title: Alphabet added to Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon
items.content: Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

items.title: MSCI keeps South Korea as emerging market, delays Indonesia review amid downgrade risk
items.content: The review comes amid hopes that Seoul could be included in MSCI's Developed Markets watchlist.

items.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
items.content: Though the firm's manufacturing index ran better than expected for June, it came largely from an inventory rebuild and despite sharp job cuts.

items.title: FedEx posts strong earnings results in last quarter with freight business
items.content: FedEx posted strong fiscal fourth quarter earnings on Tuesday in the company's last quarter that included the freight business before its spin-off.

items.title: An off-grid power project gets a major proof of concept. What it means for GE Vernova
items.content: AI data centers powered by off-grid power plants took a major step forward this week, a good sign for GE Vernova as industry challenges pile up.

items.title: The biggest wealth transfer in history is here: How the next generation will spend the trillions
items.content: The greatest wealth transfer is underway, and heirs who are set to inherit are preparing to use the money very differently from the generations that built it.

items.title: PGA Tour CEO Brian Rolapp unveils sweeping changes to professional golf
items.content: Changes to the PGA Tour are designed to elevate competition and raise payouts for winners.

items.title: House passes affordable housing bill, sends it to Trump's desk
items.content: The House on Tuesday gave final passage to a bill aimed at lowering costs for homebuyers and reining in private equity.

items.title: Senate backs Iran war powers resolution as GOP pressure grows on Trump's deal to end war
items.content: Senate Republicans want more details on Trump’s Iran deal as questions swirl around sanctions relief, nuclear restrictions and congressional approval.

items.title: Bill Gates testimony on Jeffrey Epstein ties released by House oversight panel
items.content: "I should never have met with Epstein in the first place," Bill Gates told members of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee.

items.title: CFTC sues Kentucky over actions against prediction markets, making it first red state to face federal scrutiny
items.content: The commission has now sued nine states in its battle to defend what it sees as its exclusive right to regulate event contracts.

items.title: Inside Kevin Warsh's selection process for the next Atlanta Fed president
items.content: Choosing a new head for the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta is an opportunity for new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh to reshape the central bank.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan to begin for ships stranded in Persian Gulf, maritime organization says
items.content: More than 11,000 seafarers stuck in the Persian Gulf will begin to exit through the Hormuz Strait in a large-scale evacuation plan backed by Iran and the U.S.

items.title: Traders are loving this cheap way to make big bets against chip stocks
items.content: Just a day after making new all-time records, the semiconductor sector is down almost 7% and traders are finding a cheap way to bet on a bigger pivot.

items.title: Meta announces new smart glasses starting at $299, as Zuckerberg keeps pushing wearables
items.content: Meta executives have said they see the lightweight smart glasses as a step towards a more advanced device that includes screens in the lenses.

items.title: Meta is building a prediction markets app. These stocks fell in response
items.content: The report said that CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed staff to develop a prediction markets platform, internally known as "Arena."

items.title: Sen. Cassidy plans to push 'big idea' for Social Security reform in last days in office
items.content: As Social Security faces an imminent funding shortfall, Sen. Bill Cassidy said he has come up with a plan to invest in the stock market on the program's behalf.

items.title: Progressive Brad Lander’s surging bid for Congress splinters Democrats' labor union alliance
items.content: Rep. Dan Goldman, D-N.Y., is fighting for his political life in a race with Brad Lander, who is backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt.
